concept.title: GUIDES-35438: Error when deleting two or more columns from a table
concept.shortdesc: Deleting multiple columns from a table in the Web Editor can leave a column in an abnormal state after the delete operation.

section.title: Problem
section.p: When the user selects two columns on the right side of a table, then uses the context menu DeleteColumns, the columns are removed, but the UI can leave column 1 in an inconsistent or abnormal state (per reporter; screenshot referenced in Jira).

section.title: Verbatim reporter description
section.note: Jira description (abridged punctuation): If you select the two columns on the right in the table with 6 rows and 5 rows, right-click and select DeleteColumns, the columns will be deleted, but column 1 will be deleted normally, and column 1 will remain in an abnormal form as shown in the figure below.
section.p: The phrase 6 rows and 5 rows is treated here as five columns and six table rows (one header row plus five body rows). If your reproduction uses six body rows instead, add one more row in tbody.
The wording column 1 … column 1 is ambiguous (likely first column vs. a duplicated column artifact); the screenshot in Jira clarifies the visual defect.

section.title: Sample table matching the reported grid
section.p: Use this structure as a DITA analogue of the reporter’s table. In the Web Editor, select the two rightmost columns (here: Col 4 and Col 5 for every body row), then DeleteColumns.

table.title: Five columns, six rows (1 header + 5 body) — before deleting the two right columns

section.p: After deletion, only three logical columns should remain (Col 1–3). The defect is that the editor can leave the first column or layout in an inconsistent state (per reporter).

p.xref: Original sample topic URL from Jira (external)

section.title: Engineering notes
section.p: Flagged as a candidate to be addressed with the new editor; tracked in the engineering spreadsheet (per Jira comments).

section.title: Verification
section.p: Verification on the new editor (build 5.2.0.6109, branch develop, built 2026-01-15) reported not able to reproduce the issue.
